title: When She Comes Home
content:
When she comes home again! A thousand ways
     I fashion, to myself, the tenderness
     Of my glad welcome: I shall tremble - yes;
    And touch her, as when first in the old days
    I touched her girlish hand, nor dared upraise
     Mine eyes, such was my faint heart's sweet distress.
     Then silence: And the perfume of her dress:
    The room will sway a little, and a haze
     Cloy eyesight - soulsight, even - for a space:
    And tears - yes; and the ache here in the throat,
     To know that I so ill deserve the place
    Her arms make for me; and the sobbing note
     I stay with kisses, ere the tearful face
     Again is hidden in the old embrace.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, James Whitcomb Riley delivers a powerful performance in "When She Comes Home"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
